questions.question_en: How did you and Janice keep in touch from overseas — letters, phone calls, telegrams?
questions.why_it_matters: Pre-text-era courtship logistics with Janice is core to a 1950s-60s memoir. Letters, phone calls, telegrams across an ocean are a story most narrators have rich detail on.

questions.question_en: How did the choice between waiting and pivoting actually feel at the time?
questions.why_it_matters: Pivot-under-constraint decisions shape the next decades. The narrator's calculus at the moment is information memoir summaries lose.

questions.question_en: How did the courier route end up turning into the next assignment?
questions.why_it_matters: A temporary substitution that became a permanent career pivot is the bridge moment of the chapter.

questions.question_en: What kind of photography did the Brigade need you to do?
questions.why_it_matters: The day-to-day work of an Army photographer is the specific texture of a role pivot. What got photographed, for whom, and why.
